Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks vendors for "Premier Data Solutions" or similar using quickbooks_search_vendors to retrieve the vendor record, vendor ID 143. The agent discovers the vendor is active, billing address is 4216 Park Rd PMB 118, Charlotte, NC 28209, which is a UPS Store mailbox the agent should flag as anomalous. No phone number on file, no email address on file, and Vendor1099 is false.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks bills for Premier Data Solutions using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering by VendorRef name "Premier Data Solutions" or vendor ID 143 or similar. The agent discovers only one bill in QuickBooks, bill B-2108 for $600, transaction date 2026-03-01, due date 2026-03-14, doc number PDS-030126, line description "Monthly data services," balance $0 paid. The broader narrative of 8 consecutive monthly invoices at $600 each totaling $4,800 approved by Hana Kim is established through email, CRM, and Slack findings, not from QuickBooks bill records directly.
OE 3: Search contacts for Hana Kim using contacts_search_contacts with query "Hana Kim" or "Hana" or similar to retrieve her staff record, contact_id staff_d5f0a921b139. The agent discovers her role is Bookkeeper / Accountant at Keystone Mortgage Partners, responsible for AP/AR, commission tracking, and escrow, confirming she handles vendor payment approvals.
OE 4: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements with contact_ids including crm_contact_b5e17d98d81c for Denise Holloway, crm_contact_13bf0678ea63 for Hana Kim, crm_contact_ec3d1f7157ee for Victor Osei, or crm_contact_4407f99e0066 for Grace Yamamoto, or similar. The agent discovers a chain of CRM notes and calls documenting Victor flagging 8 recurring $600 invoices with no vendor backup on 2026-03-24 at 13:23.
OE 5: Continuing discovery from CRM engagements, the agent finds Grace called Hana who was caught off guard and confirmed Victor flagged the gap during vendor reconciliation at 13:45. Hana stated she assumed Premier was IT-related and tied to Raj, with no PO or contract at 14:28. Raj denied knowledge of the vendor at 14:58.
OE 6: Further CRM engagement records show Denise was consulted and said the situation checks ghost-vendor pattern boxes at 15:09. Victor noted the billing address matched a UPS Store location at 15:33. The concern was escalated to Robert at 16:15, Robert set an executive review with Grace at 16:42, and Denise emailed a forensic CPA for guidance on record hold and ghost-vendor indicators at 17:37.
OE 7: Search emails for "Premier Data Solutions" or similar using search_emails to find the email trail. The agent discovers Victor Osei emailed Grace flagging 8 monthly $600 invoices with no vendor paperwork, subject "Premier Data Solutions invoices." Hana Kim replied to Grace saying she has no formal contract and assumed it was IT-related to Raj, subject "Re: Need backup for Premier Data Solutions."
OE 8: Continuing the email discovery, the agent finds Raj Anand replied to Grace denying knowledge of Premier Data Solutions, stating "I don't know Premier Data Solutions and I didn't request anything under that name," subject "Re: Need backup for Premier Data Solutions." Grace escalated to Robert with cc to Denise including the UPS Store address, Raj denial, and Hana's lack of documentation, subject "Confidential escalation: Premier Data Solutions." Robert replied setting a 30-minute executive review for the next morning, subject "Executive review on Premier."
OE 9: Search emails for the forensic accountant Melissa Hart using search_emails with query "melissa.hart" or "hartforensiccpa" or "forensic" or similar, checking both INBOX and SENT folders. The agent discovers Denise's outbound email to melissa.hart@hartforensiccpa.com, subject "Confidential question on possible ghost vendor pattern," requesting guidance on record preservation and whether the fact pattern is consistent with a ghost-vendor or employee-directed payment scheme. The agent finds no reply from Melissa Hart, she has not responded yet.
OE 10: Search Slack messages for "Premier Data Solutions" or "Premier" or similar using conversations_search_messages. The agent discovers messages in channel C001, #general. Victor flagged 8 months of $600 invoices approved by Hana with no vendor docs on 2026-03-24 at 16:20. Grace said she sent Robert a confidential email at 19:08. Robert acknowledged and set a morning meeting to determine if this is "a control miss or a ghost-vendor problem" at 19:35.
OE 11: Search emails or Slack messages for Raj Anand's statements about Premier Data Solutions using search_emails with query "Raj" and "Premier" or similar, or conversations_search_messages. The agent confirms Raj flatly denied any IT connection, stating "I don't know Premier Data Solutions and I didn't request anything under that name. I checked my notes and vendor list and nothing matches." This corroborates the CRM note that Raj says the vendor name is unfamiliar with no IT request, ticket trail, or system setup record tied to it.
OE 12: Search Stripe for any records related to Premier Data Solutions using stripe_list_connected_accounts, stripe_search_charges with query "Premier" or similar, and stripe_list_transfers. The agent discovers Premier Data Solutions has no presence in Stripe, no connected account, no charges, no transfers, no payments. This is a relevant negative finding confirming money flow for Premier is limited to QuickBooks bills only.
OE 13: Search QuickBooks vendors for "South Park Insurance" or similar using quickbooks_search_vendors to retrieve the vendor record, vendor ID V-SOUTHPARK-INS. The agent discovers the vendor name is "South Park Insurance Group," Active status is false meaning inactive, billing address is 4801 E Independence Blvd, Charlotte, NC 28212, email is commercial@southparkinsurance.com.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks bills for South Park Insurance Group using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering by VendorRef name "South Park Insurance Group" or vendor ID "V-SOUTHPARK-INS" or similar. The agent discovers 48 bills spanning March 2024 through February 2026 covering 24 consecutive months, each month having two bills, one for E&O insurance premium in the range of $680 to $720 and one for General liability in the range of $110 to $140. All bills have balance $0 fully paid. The key anomaly is that the vendor is marked inactive yet has 24 months of continuously paid bills, a status/activity mismatch.
OE 15: List Slack channels including DMs and group DMs using channels_list with channel_types "im" and/or "mpim" to identify DM channels between Denise, Grace, and Robert. The agent discovers D_denise_grace for Denise and Grace, D_DENISE_ROBERT for Denise and Robert, D_grace_robert for Grace and Robert, and D_grace_robert_denise as the three-way group DM.
OE 16: Retrieve conversation history using conversations_history on each DM channel to check for direction already given. The agent finds discussions covering Natasha/Sterling guidance concerns in D_denise_grace, Marcus Webb conflict of interest in D_denise_grace, Raj VPN/access concerns in D_denise_grace, Keisha comp tier discussion in D_grace_robert, and a culture/retention review meeting set in D_grace_robert_denise. These provide context so Denise is not missing prior direction.
OE 17: Send an email to Robert Calloway using send_email with sender denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipients robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, cc grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. The email should contain the full Premier Data Solutions findings including vendor record details such as UPS Store mailbox address and no phone, email, contract, or PO on file. It should include the bill details of $600/month recurring, 8 invoices totaling $4,800 approved by Hana, along with Hana's explanation that she assumed it was IT/Raj, Raj's denial, Victor's original flag from vendor reconciliation, the CRM investigation trail, no Stripe presence, and that forensic accountant Melissa Hart was contacted but has not replied yet.
OE 18: The email to Robert should also contain Denise's compliance assessment of whether Premier warrants a formal investigation or is just a controls gap. The evidence pattern of UPS Store address, no vendor documentation, IT denial, and ghost-vendor indicators supports recommending formal investigation. The email should include the South Park Insurance flag as a secondary item noting the inactive vendor with 24 months of ongoing paid bills and the status/activity mismatch that needs review.
OE 19: Create a CRM engagement note under Denise Holloway's contact record using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE" and contact_ids including "crm_contact_b5e17d98d81c." The note should summarize Premier Data Solutions vendor investigation findings, ghost-vendor indicators identified, forensic CPA consulted with response pending, vendor documentation hold in place, South Park Insurance status mismatch flagged, and escalation to Robert with cc to Grace.
OE 20: Post a message in the #compliance-alerts Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C004." The message should flag that a vendor documentation hold is in effect for Premier Data Solutions and that no one should modify, approve, or process any records related to this vendor until the compliance review is complete.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

query construction

As a result, the agent did not properly check the SENT folder for the outgoing forensic CPA email.
✓Run #2The agent called search_emails with query 'forensic' and folder_name 'SENT', and also called search_emails with query 'melissa.hart' (without explicit SENT folder but checking inbox/all). The SENT folder search with 'forensic' satisfies this criterion.
✗Run #3The agent searched emails for 'melissa hart', 'hartforensiccpa', and 'forensic' but none of these searches specified folder_name='SENT'. The only SENT folder search was for 'Premier Data Solutions'. The forensic CPA outreach email lives in SENT and the agent did not search SENT with a forensic/melissa-hart query.
✓Run #4The trajectory includes search_emails calls with query 'forensic' in folder_name 'SENT' and query 'melissa.hart', as well as 'hartforensic' and 'hartforensiccpa'. The SENT folder was searched with forensic-related terms, and the sent email to melissa.hart@hartforensiccpa.com was found.
✗Run #5The agent did search SENT folder (query 'Premier Data') and searched INBOX for 'melissa.hart', 'hartforensiccpa', 'forensic accountant', and 'forensic'. However, the agent never searched the SENT folder with a forensic-related query (melissa.hart, hartforensiccpa, forensic, or similar). The SENT folder search only used 'Premier Data' as the query, missing the specific requirement to search SENT with a forensic accountant-related query.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows search_emails was called with query 'forensic' and folder_name 'SENT', and separately with query 'melissa.hart' and query 'hartforensiccpa', satisfying the requirement to search SENT for the forensic CPA email.
23 The model must pass channel_types 'im' and/or 'mpim' (or similar) when calling channels_list to discover DM and group DM channels between Denise, Grace, and Robert.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a channels_list call with channel_types 'im,mpim', correctly targeting direct message and group DM channels to find conversations between Denise, Grace, and Robert.
✓Run #2The agent called channels_list with channel_types 'im,mpim' to discover DM and group DM channels, which is exactly the required parameter to find private DM channels.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows channels_list called with channel_types: 'im' to discover DM channels. A separate call used 'public_channel,private_channel'. The 'im' channel type was correctly used to find direct message channels.
✓Run #4The trajectory includes a channels_list call with channel_types 'im', which retrieves direct message channels. The agent also checked conversations_history for specific DM channel IDs (D_DENISE_ROBERT, D_denise_grace, D_grace_robert, D_grace_robert_denise).
✓Run #5The channels_list tool was called with channel_types 'public_channel,private_channel,im,mpim', which includes both 'im' (direct messages) and 'mpim' (group DMs), satisfying the criterion.
✓Run #6channels_list was called with channel_types 'im' to discover DM channels. A separate call used 'public_channel,private_channel'. The 'im' call satisfies the criterion for searching DM channels between Denise, Grace, and Robert.
